reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output explicitly notes that 35 sections were omitted due to a token budget. This results in the complete loss of content from pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42 of the source document, including the FCC Fact Sheet, the majority of the NPRM text, and legislative updates for House Bill 213.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source contains content on pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42 (FCC documents, HB 213, NMAC info).

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Aside from the omitted pages, text within included sections is also truncated. In Section 9 (Introduction to NPRM), the first paragraph is cut off mid-sentence after 'Section 902 of the new legislation requires the', losing the remainder of the introductory text from page 9 of the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Section 902 of the new legislation requires the Commission to take action to help address the diversion of 911 fees by states and other jurisdictions for purposes unrelated to 911. (p. 9, paragraph 1)
concerns.output_quote: Section 902 of the new legislation requires the

concerns.analysis: Multiple tables in Section 46 (KXLY Invoice) are structurally corrupted. In tables 591, 593, 595, and 598, the label 'Spots:' from the source was incorrectly included as a column header. This caused a column misalignment where data is shifted relative to its correct header (e.g., the spot number '1' appears under the 'Spots:' header, and the channel 'KXLY' appears under the '#' header). Additionally, Table 596 incorrectly places data values into the header row rather than the table body.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 46: Spots: # Ch Day Air Date Air Time Description Start/End Time Length Ad-ID Rate Type (headers for spot detail)

concerns.analysis: The agenda list in the Minutes (Section 2, index 20) was converted to an unordered list, and the item numbers (1, 2, 3) present in the source were removed. This results in a loss of sequential meaning.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2: 1. Chair Opening Remarks... 2. Roll Call...
concerns.output_quote: Index 20: list_type: unordered, items: ["Chair Opening Remarks...", "Roll Call..."]
